Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. The plant sprouts and grows with this Pokémon.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: There is a plant bulb on its back. When it absorbs nutrients, the bulb is said to blossom into a large flower.

Name: Venusaur
Ability-Effect: Each basic [G] Energy attached to your Pokémon provides [G][G] Energy. You can’t apply more than 1 Jungle Totem Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: By spreading the broad petals of its flower and catching the sun’s rays, it fills its body with power.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

Name: Breloom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

Name: Virizion
Effects: You may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

Name: Shining Genesect
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [G] Energy from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma. They upgraded the cannon on its back.

Name: Entei-GX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s High-Pressure Heat attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Coal is the source of Torkoal’s energy. Large amounts of coal can be found in the mountains where they live.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: Said to have been born from the sun, it spews fire from its horns and encases itself in a cocoon of fire when it evolves.

Name: Volcarona
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A sea of fire engulfs the surroundings of their battles, since they use their six wings to scatter their ember scales.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.

Name: Litten
Flavor-Text: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

Name: Torracat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire.

Name: Incineroar
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 80 more damage.
Discard all [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it’s not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer’s orders with complete nonchalance.

Name: Totodile
Flavor-Text: It is small but rough and tough. It won’t hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

Name: Croconaw
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may move all Energy from your Active Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

Name: Qwilfish
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It swims by rotating its two tails like a screw. When it dives, its flotation sac collapses.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live in a gap in the spatial dimension parallel to ours. It appears in mythology.

Name: Manaphy
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any [W] Energy attached to it.
Flavor-Text: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: Put a [W] Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It crosses the world, running over the surfaces of oceans and rivers. It appears at scenic waterfronts.

Name: Shining Volcanion
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It’s said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

Name: Raichu-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [L] Energy attached to your Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Voltorb
Flavor-Text: It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

Name: Electrode
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of “The Bomb Ball.”

Name: Raikou
Effects: Attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The rain clouds it carries let it fire thunderbolts at will. They say that it descended with lightning.

Name: Plusle
Effects: If Minun is on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

Name: Minun
Effects: Draw a card.
This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with lightning. It assists those who want to build an ideal world.

Name: Ekans
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The older it gets, the longer it grows. At night, it wraps its long body around tree branches to rest.

Name: Arbok
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

Name: Jynx
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.

Name: Mewtwo-GX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shining Mew
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

Name: Latios
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

Name: Shining Jirachi
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting all of the Evolution cards on it into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Generations have believed that any wish written on a note on its head will come true when it awakens.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

Name: Marshadow
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may have each player shuffle their hand into their deck and draw 4 cards.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Able to conceal itself in shadows, it never appears before humans, so its very existence was the stuff of myth.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Active Pokémon can’t retreat.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.

Name: Zorua
Flavor-Text: It changes so it looks like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.

Name: Zoroark-GX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When this legendary Pokémon’s wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures.

Name: Hoopa
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX.
Flavor-Text: In its true form, it possess a huge amount of power. Legends of its avarice tell how it once carried off an entire castle to gain the treasure hidden within.

Name: Shining Rayquaza
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the ozone layer far above the clouds and cannot be seen from the ground.

Name: Shining Arceus
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent’s attacks.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: According to the legends of Sinnoh, this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world.

Name: Damage Mover
Description: Move 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Great Ball
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Hau
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lillie
Description: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. If it’s your first turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Breeder
Description: Draw 2 cards and heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you have no cards in your deck, you can’t play this card.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Sophocles
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Super Scoop Up
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Warp Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch that Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Entei-GX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
